draft.title: a minimalist journal app
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a minimalist journal app

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: A minimalist journal app benefits from rounded, approachable edges rather than sharp geometric forms
facade.label: Soft corners
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward approachable, gentle aesthetics. Likely prefers curved UI elements, smooth interactions, less austere feeling.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers precision and clarity through defined edges. May favor clean lines, geometric layouts, more structured/minimal appearance.

evidence.hypothesis: A minimalist journal app benefits from rounded, approachable edges rather than sharp geometric forms
evidence.implication: User gravitates toward approachable, gentle aesthetics. Likely prefers curved UI elements, smooth interactions, less austere feeling.

facade.hypothesis: Users building minimalist journals may be uncertain whether UI should feel sharp and clean (digital precision) or gently blurred (approachable warmth)
facade.label: Soft edges
facade.acceptImplies: The journal app should feel approachable and slightly tactile, with rounded corners, gentle shadows, and a warm but minimal aesthetic—less stark geometric precision
facade.rejectImplies: The journal app should lean toward crisp, sharp-cornered UI with high contrast and clean lines—digital clarity over organic warmth

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer animated entry transitions over instant appearance, creating a sense of time passing and mindful moment capture
facade.axisTargeted: Motion: animated transitions vs instant rendering
facade.label: Entries slide in smoothly
facade.acceptImplies: User values fluidity and temporal awareness; journal feels like a living, breathing space where moments accumulate gracefully
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers snappy, immediate feedback; animation might feel slow or precious for a minimalist journal focused on quick capture

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer understated, self-assured tone over either bubbly cheerfulness or clinical sterility
facade.axisTargeted: Voice and Personality — professional warmth vs neutral efficiency
facade.label: Quiet confidence
facade.acceptImplies: The app should use calm, assured language with minimal exclamation points or emoji; feels like a thoughtful friend, not a startup or enterprise tool
facade.rejectImplies: User leans toward either more playful/energetic voice OR more minimal/austere tone; the middle ground of 'confident but warm' isn't the right fit

facade.hypothesis: Minimalist journals work best when showing ONE key metric (entries written, words, mood score) rather than dense dashboards with multiple stats
facade.axisTargeted: Data density — single metric vs multi-stat dashboard
facade.label: One number per day
facade.acceptImplies: User believes restraint in metrics drives focus and clarity. Favors sparse, high-signal design over comprehensive tracking.
facade.rejectImplies: User wants a richer view of their journal practice — multiple dimensions visible at once. May value context, trends, or comparative metrics over single-point simplicity.

facade.hypothesis: For a minimalist journal, users might prefer stacked sidebar navigation (date list, tag filter, settings) rather than top tabs or floating menus, reducing cognitive load and keeping writing space central.
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure (sidebar vs tabs vs floating UI)
facade.label: Sidebar + sidebar + sidebar
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward left-edge persistent navigation; minimalism thrives with edge-anchored controls, not centered UI.
facade.rejectImplies: User resists sidebar stacking; prefers minimal nav (maybe just one drawer) or tabs; values screen real estate more than persistent access patterns.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal visual design (clean, sparse) vs. design with subtle visual richness (textures, depth, color)
Whether user wants linear, chronological entry flow vs. flexible, non-linear organization and navigation
Whether user expects a neutral, invisible interface vs. one with gentle guidance, encouragement, or personality
Whether user seeks a calm, meditative journaling space vs. one that feels energizing or motivating
Whether user wants entries to be simple text-only vs. entries that support tags, dates, or light metadata
Whether user prefers instant, snappy interactions vs. smooth, gentle transitions and animations

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal visual design (clean, sparse) vs. design with subtle visual richness (textures, depth, color)
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants linear, chronological entry flow vs. flexible, non-linear organization and navigation
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user expects a neutral, invisible interface vs. one with gentle guidance, encouragement, or personality
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user seeks a calm, meditative journaling space vs. one that feels energizing or motivating
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants entries to be simple text-only vs. entries that support tags, dates, or light metadata
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers instant, snappy interactions vs. smooth, gentle transitions and animations
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: A minimalist journal app would benefit from a single-color or grayscale visual system rather than multiple colors, reinforcing restraint and focus on content
facade.label: Monochrome palette
facade.acceptImplies: User values extreme visual restraint and believes color distracts from written reflection. Likely prefers black/white/gray only, or single accent color maximum.
facade.rejectImplies: User wants some color presence to create warmth or visual interest. Minimalism doesn't mean absolute grayscale. Likely seeking color restraint rather than color elimination.

draft.title: Daily Reflection
draft.summary: A minimalist journal app for capturing daily thoughts with a clean, distraction-free interface. Focus on simplicity and ease of entry.
draft.html:
Daily Reflection

Today, December 19

Recent Entries
December 18
Feeling reflective today. Spent time thinking about what matters most and realized I've been rushing through moments.
December 17
Good day overall. Made progress on the project and had a meaningful conversation with a friend.
